rulings.Case Title: BRASIL SF, LLC, DBA BOSSA NOVA VS. AINA ENTERPRISES, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Issue, Evidence And/Or Terminating Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday May 5, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT AINA ENTERPRISES, LLC'S Motion For Issue, Evidence And/Or Terminating Sanctions. Pro Tem Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is DENIED, without prejudice. A corporation whose powers have been suspended for nonpayment of the corporate franchise tax lacks capacity to sue in California courts. Rev. & Tax.C. § 23301; Reed v. Norman (1957) 48 C2d 338, 342. Plaintiff Brasil SF, LLC, therefore, lacks capacity to sue in this Court. However, issue and/or evidentiary sanctions cannot be imposed without either filing a copy of the written discovery or a separate statement under CRC 3.1345(a)(7) that shows the issues raised in the written discovery are relevant to the allegations made in the complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: COX, CASTLE & NICHOLSON et al VS. ACCO ENGINEERED SYSTEMS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Good Faith Settlement (Control Date)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 5, 2015, Line 3, Motion For Good Faith Settlement (Control Date). The matter is taken Off Calendar. Duplicate entry. =(HK/302)

rulings.Case Title: COX, CASTLE & NICHOLSON et al VS. ACCO ENGINEERED SYSTEMS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Joint Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 5, 2015, Line 3, CROSS COMPLAINANT ACCO ENGINEERED SYSTEMS, INC. Joint Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement. The motion is denied without prejudice because there is no substantial evidence regarding the settlors' proportionate liability. "The party asserting the lack of good faith shall have the burden of proof on that issue." CCP 877.6(d). However, once the contestor attacks the settlement as lacking good faith, settlors were required to file counteraffidavits to make an evidentiary showing that the settlement was "in the ballpark." Settlors must provide substantial evidence to support a critical assumption as to the nature and extent of its liability. See Mattco Forge, Inc. v. Arthur Young & Co. (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1350, n. 6. "If¿there is no substantial evidence to support a critical assumption as to the nature and extent of a settling defendant's liability, then a determination of good faith based upon such assumption is an abuse of discretion." Toyota Motor Sales USA, Inc. v. Sup. Crt. (1990) 220 Cal.App.3d 864, 871. In this case, ACCO's moving papers rely on the declaration of counsel (Mr. Osier) and the declaration of Mr. Ryan from BANA. AR&B correctly objects to the Osier declaration based on hearsay. Mr. Osier lacks personal knowledge regarding the repair history at the premises. The Ryan dec., which is unobjected to, substantiates BANA's claim for approximately $10,000,000 in damages. AR&B's opposition correctly notes that there is no substantial evidence regarding the settlors' proportionate liability and ACCO's reply evidence fails to cure the defect. The declaration from Mr. Spohn merely sets forth the discovery history between BANA and AR&B. The reply declaration of Mr. Osier similarly fails to present substantial evidence about the nature and extent of the settlors' liability. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(HK/302)

rulings.Case Title: HENRY M BURGOYNE III VS. GOLDSTERINRIGHT, A CALIFORNIA PROFESSIONAL CORPOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendants Goldsteinenright Accountancy Corporations And David Woos Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 5, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANT GOLDSTEINENRIGHT ACCOUNTANCY CORPORATION, DAVID WOO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendants Goldsteinenright Accountancy Corporations And David Woos Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication. Denied. Defendants have not met their initial burden of production. Defendants argue that Plaintiff's discovery responses reveal that he does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence to establish causation and/or damages. Defendants first contend that Plaintiff's responses to special interrogatories are factually void. Defendants do not submit verified responses. "Unsworn responses are tantamount to no responses at all." (Appleton v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 636.) Because Defendants rely on unverified responses, Defendants fail to shift the burden. Defendants next contend that Plaintiff has failed to produce any documents evidencing his claims. Defendants' attorney submits a declaration stating: "I have reviewed documents produced by Burgyone that are bates-labeled Confidential 000001-006632 and HB Confidential 000001-002319 in connection with discovery in this action. In so doing, I did not see any documents evidencing that an affirmative vote of seventy-five (75%) of the partners in Burgoyne's former law firm [KB Firm] making Burgoyne a 50% partner in the Law Firm ever took place. I also did not see any documentary evidence showing that tax authorities ever asked Burgyone to pay more taxes and/or penalties as a result of Defendants' reporting his interest in the Law Firm at 45% instead of 50%." (Declaration of Diana Fung, par. 5.) This is also insufficient to shift the burden. Defendants undisputed material facts do not provide that any amendment to the partnership agreement must be in writing. Accordingly, it is insufficient to state that Defendants' counsel has not seen any document evidencing the vote. Additionally, Defendants' counsel's statement that she did not see any documents about increased tax liabilities is insufficient to shift the burden, as the damages sought in the Second Amended Complaint are not limited to tax liabilities. (Second Amended Complaint, par. 32.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(HK/302)

rulings.Case Title: PRECISION INDUSTRIAL CONSTRUCTORS, INC. VS. SNOWFLAKE MILL INVESTORS, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Smis And Rabins Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, And For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp Sections 2023.030(A) And 2030.300(D)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 5, 2015, Line 7, DEFENDANT RABIN WORLDWIDE, INC., SNOWFLAKE MILL INVESTORS, LLC, RYAN SMITH Smis And Rabins Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, And For Sanctions Pursuant To CCP Sections 2023.030(A) And 2030.300(D). The motion and the request for sanctions are denied. The principal complaint of the moving parties is that while the responses identify categories of damages sustained, Plaintiff has not stated the dollar amount of each category of damages identified. However, the plain wording of the interrogatories do not ask for specific dollar amounts of damages; they inquire instead as to "what damages¿ have [been] suffered" and supporting facts. The responses adequately and in some detail identify the categories of damages claimed to have been suffered and the factual basis for the claim that damages have been incurred. rulings.Case Title: STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INS. CO. VS. GARRETT BAUMGARTNER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 5, 2015, Line 8, DEFENDANT GARRETT BAUMGARTNER Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment. The motion is granted. The Court vacates the default and default judgment. Defendant demonstrates that he was not properly substitute served. See CCP 415.20(b); CCP 473(d). In addition, even if service were proper, defendant demonstrates that he lacked actual notice of the suit in time to defend. See CCP 473.5. Defendant to file and serve his answer by May 7, 2015. rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH FULLER VS. THAD DAVIS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Thad Davis, Sarah Zenewicz, And Ropes Gray Llps Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Pursuant To C.C.P., Â§ 425.16 [Anti-Slapp Motion]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 5, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT THAD DAVIS, SARAH ZENEWICZ, ROPES & GRAY LLP Motion To Strike Pursuant To C.C.P., Sec. 425.16 [Anti-Slapp Motion]. Granted. The anti-SLAPP motion is timely filed. CCP sec. 425.16(f) provides that an anti-SLAPP motion "may be filed within 60 days of the service of the complaint¿" A defendant can waive service by making a general appearance. (CCP sec. 410.50(a).) A motion to strike constitutes a general appearance. (CCP sec. 1014.) Accordingly, "service" and the filing of the anti-SLAPP motion effectively occurred simultaneously. Additionally, the use of the word "within" indicates that the 60 day requirement sets forth the outer limit for filing the motion and does not "fix the first point in time." (Glass v. Benkert (1971) 18 Cal.App.3d 322, 327.) Lastly, the purpose of the anti-SLAPP statute is to "enable the defendant-victim of a SLAPP suit to extricate himself or herself from the lawsuit as quickly and inexplicably as possible." (S.B. Beach Properties v. Berti (2006) 39 Cal.4th 374, 382.) Requiring a defendant to wait until the complaint is served on him to file an anti-SLAPP motion would run afoul to this policy. Because the motion was timely filed, the Court reaches the merits of the motion. The Court must engage in a two-step process when ruling on an Anti-SLAPP motion under CCP §425.16. "First, the court decides whether the defendant has made a threshold showing that the cause of action is one arising from protected activity¿If the court finds such a showing has been made¿it then determines whether the plaintiff has demonstrated a probability of prevailing on the claim." (Equilon Enterprises v. Consumer Cause Inc. (2002) 29 Cal.4th 53, 67.) The first prong is met if the moving party meets the burden of making a prima facie showing that the plaintiff's cause of action arises out of an act in furtherance of a person's right to petition or free speech under the U.S. or California Constitutions in connection with a public issue. (CCP § 425.16(b)(1).) The entire complaint arises from Defendants' right to petition on behalf of their clients and therefore the first prong is met here. (See Cabral v. Martins (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 471, 479-80.) Plaintiff has not demonstrated a probability of prevailing and therefore the motion is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(HK/302)

rulings.Case Title: KIMBERLEE HAMPTON VS. LIBERTY LIFE ASSURANCE COMPANY OF BOSTON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 5, 2015, Line 13, DEFENDANT LIBERTY LIFE ASSURANCE COMPANY OF BOSTON DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled with ten days to answer. Liberty is a private party, not a governmental agency, and the prefiling requirement that it seeks to impose on plaintiff is to be determined by Liberty itself, not the UC or any other government, so the doctrine of exhaustion of administrative remedies is inapplicable. While a private party contract can impose a prefiling requirement, the language of the parties' contract shows that an appeal of Liberty's denial of benefits to plaintiff is permissive, not obligatory.
